@@
A. Freeform text summary of the failure:
After intersecting NYSE Arca ETFs with available price tables, the agent only checked a small sample of tickers (e.g., SPY, IVV, VOO, etc. via multiple query_db calls showing max_adj < 200) and, after a failed execute_python attempt to build a comprehensive UNION query, prematurely returned “0” without evaluating the full set.
B. Failure modes encountered:
FM1: no
FM2: yes
FM3: no
FM4: no
@@

A. Freeform text summary of the failure:
After pulling NYSE Arca ETF tickers and repeatedly failing to generate a batch SQL to check 2015 max adjusted closes (execute_python syntax errors), the agent tested only a few symbols (e.g., SPY, IVV, GLD) and prematurely concluded zero ETFs met the criterion, stopping before scanning all tickers and without mapping to ETF names.

@@
A. Freeform text summary of the failure:
After correctly identifying NYSE Arca ETF symbols and intersecting them with available trade tables, the agent attempted to build a massive UNION ALL SQL in execute_python but repeatedly hit SyntaxError (e.g., call_GWLxqBNfKcV6UTTc8XlzCaql, call_TPONv1qTfjlNZPxDBMs34zuu, call_JcB0i7k8TImZoFjbGGfWuhih) and then prematurely returned an apology instead of executing the needed query.
B. Failure modes encountered:
FM1: no

FM3: no
FM4: yes
@@

@@
A. Freeform text summary of the failure:
The agent had a correct approach but implemented it incorrectly—repeatedly generating invalid SQL strings in execute_python (multiple SyntaxErrors), issuing an empty DuckDB query (call_ta1svCGSXJY2s3QiELv2lClG), and testing a non-existent table "BRK" (call_CAJcykgv05dUoYwnLWAGVw1C)—leading it to return an error message instead of the requested ETF list and count.
B. Failure modes encountered:
